citations.description: Ticket comment by Joe Oakhart: 'Landed in Gerrit change 869. AGENTS.md now tells Codex sessions to prepend the current worktree .venv/bin on every command, following docs/local-dev-env.md, so make lint and make typecheck use repository tooling. Verified and LLM-reviewed before merge.' — names no run ID, log line, or timestamp specific to the lint gate result.

findings.criterion: make lint exits zero in the correctly configured worktree.
findings.detail: This criterion requires a runtime gate outcome (make lint exiting 0). It is untagged and therefore classified as codebase-verifiable, but no codebase evidence can demonstrate a command exit code. The ticket comment ('Verified and LLM-reviewed before merge') is a vague attestation with no verifiable specifics (no run ID, log output, CI link, or timestamp). The criterion cannot be satisfied from repository inspection alone.
findings.remediation: Tag this criterion [operator-attested] and record a ticket comment naming the specific Gerrit change run URL (or CI Actions run link), the observed exit code / log output, and the timestamp when make lint was verified to exit zero in the worktree.

citations.description: Ticket comment by Joe Oakhart: 'Landed in Gerrit change 869. AGENTS.md now tells Codex sessions to prepend the current worktree .venv/bin on every command, following docs/local-dev-env.md, so make lint and make typecheck use repository tooling. Verified and LLM-reviewed before merge.' — names no run ID, log line, or timestamp specific to the typecheck gate result.

findings.criterion: make typecheck exits zero in the correctly configured worktree.
findings.detail: Same as the lint criterion: a runtime gate outcome that cannot be verified from the codebase. The ticket comment is a vague general attestation without the specifics required (run ID, log lines, timestamp) to credit it even as an operator-attested criterion.
findings.remediation: Tag this criterion [operator-attested] and record a ticket comment naming the specific Gerrit change run URL (or CI Actions run link), the observed exit code / log output, and the timestamp when make typecheck was verified to exit zero in the worktree.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
